prompt: Abstract grid composition of nine square panels, each showing a slightly different geometric variation — a population of candidates under selection pressure. Flat vector style with subtle gradients. Muted palette of deep indigo, teal, and warm off-white. Minimal lines, no text, no literal machinery or AI imagery. Slight grain texture. Evokes iteration, refinement, and design-system evolution without being on-the-nose.
title: Automating Around a Design Skills Gap
subtitle: The older feature images on this site are rough. I'm not a designer and I'm not trying to become one. Closing the skills gap with automation, dropping the friction of shipping posts, and growing a design system by artificial selection.

notes: This is better than the previous iteration, but I wish the geometric pattern wasn't so regular.

prompt: Irregular cluster of geometric panels at varied scales and orientations — a loose population of candidates under selection pressure, not arranged on a grid. Flat vector style with subtle gradients. Muted palette of deep indigo, teal, and warm off-white. Organic spacing, overlapping shapes at slight angles, some panels fading at their edges. Minimal lines, no text, no literal machinery or AI imagery. Slight grain texture. Evokes iteration, refinement, and organic variation without being on-the-nose.
title: DOM Bake Test
subtitle: Phase 12 proof

prompt:
Abstract typographic column field, vertical columns of stacked
horizontal bars of varying length suggesting blocks of set
text without being readable, parchment-cream and muted
chartreuse tones on a deep cool ink-near-black background,
generous vertical rhythm, carefully-tuned column gutters, hand-
ruled quality to the block edges, composition weighted to the
upper two-thirds with quiet open measure below, faint grid
ruling, editorial publication page abstraction, no text no
words no letters no typography no labels no people no objects
title: Automating Around a Design Skills Gap

marks and small annotations drifting in the margins, ink-bleed
softness at each stroke edge, cool ink-near-black background,
editorial revision-pass atmosphere, sparse and intentional
placement, compositional weight in the upper half with quiet
measure below, no text no words no letters no typography no
labels no people no objects

prompt:
Abstract page-margin composition, a series of thin parallel
vertical rules in varying weights and lengths suggesting the
gutter and margin structure of a printed publication page,
parchment-cream lines on deep cool ink-near-black background,
a single signal-green chartreuse highlight line offset from
the rest, controlled asymmetric balance, compositional weight
in the upper half, faint horizontal baseline grid softening
toward the lower third, editorial publication abstraction, no
text no words no letters no typography no labels no people
no objects
title: Your Content Workflow Is Already Obsolete. Your AI Agent Is the Replacement.
subtitle: AI in content marketing is table stakes; most workflows using it aren't fast or flexible enough to keep up. The third option: stop treating AI as a feature bolted onto your workflow and start treating the agent as the workflow. An editorial calendar built on top of a coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, any of them), Markdown files, and a weekend of work.
